prompt:
Set up a Community Resilience Operations Hub that enables local agencies and partner nonprofits to run offline programs for crisis response, volunteer engagement, and benefit fulfillment from a single web portal. The Vue.js user interface provides role-based workspaces for Individual Participants, Volunteers, Rescue Stations/Organizations, and Administrators, with fast in-page feedback for approvals, status changes, and exception flags. New users register with a username and password, then complete real-name verification by entering legal name and date of birth; organizations additionally upload credential documents (PDF/JPG) for admin review before gaining publishing and fulfillment privileges. Users can switch roles only after approval, and blacklisted accounts are blocked immediately with an appeal form that routes to admin review within 3 business days. Organizations publish online or on-site events with custom registration forms, capacity limits (default 50 seats), and waitlists that auto-promote in timestamp order when cancellations occur; registrations may require manual review, with on-screen confirmations and a downloadable roster. The system also supports resource publishing where approved users can claim limited items or download files under usage limits (for example, 1 claim per household per 30 days, or 3 downloads per user per file version), with local printable notices in place of email/SMS. Reward distribution adds tiered rewards with inventory, per-user purchase limits (for example, max 2 per person), shipping address maintenance using US address formats, and fulfillment tracking for either e-vouchers or physical shipments via stored tracking numbers; overdue alerts surface on the dashboard when a reward sits in “Packed” or “Shipped” longer than 7 days without completion. An Alert Center and Work Order flow lets staff acknowledge equipment and facility faults (offline, over-temperature, leakage, overcurrent) with severity levels, dispatch tickets to on-duty personnel, capture handling notes and photos, and close with a post-incident review and SLA timing. Operations dashboards and analytics show booking/registration volume, completion and cancellation rates, staff workload, popular service categories, and retention, with filters by time range and export controls.

The backend uses Spring Boot to implement a fully offline REST-style API layer consumed by the Vue.js frontend over the local network, with MySQL for durable storage of accounts, verifications, events, registrations, resources, rewards, shipments/vouchers, alerts, tickets, and audit logs. Authentication is local-only (no third-party login), with passwords hashed using BCrypt and a lockout rule of 10 failed attempts triggering a 30-minute local lock; administrators can reset passwords after identity review. Sensitive fields such as date of birth, address lines, and uploaded credential files are encrypted at rest, while the UI masks them by default (for example, showing only city/state and last 4 of ZIP until a user has “View PII” permission). Document uploads are validated by file type and size (max 10 MB) and stored on a local disk path referenced in MySQL with checksum-based duplicate detection; no cloud scanning is assumed. Reward and inventory transitions are enforced as a state machine to prevent skipping steps, and exceptions (lost shipment, damaged item, voucher invalid) require a reason code and supervisor approval to reopen. Alert rules are configurable with thresholds and cooldown windows (for example, over-temperature above 120°F for 5 minutes, suppress repeats for 15 minutes), and SLA metrics compute first-response and time-to-close by severity. Reporting supports metric-definition management, custom report templates, and export permissions; exports generate local CSV/PDF files with row-level filters, and data-quality reports compute missing rates, anomaly distributions, and duplicate-record counts to support operations audits without any internet dependency.
